evidence.scientific_clarity: Volcano plot correctly classifies ~3000 genes into up/down/NS using data.json thresholds, draws threshold lines, and annotates top-5 by -log10p — fully satisfies the brief.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype='none' set in script; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (Custom) encoding for all three DejaVu Sans variants — text is not selectable or editable.
evidence.palette: Red/blue for up/down direction and grey for NS shows correct directional discipline; red-blue pair is reasonably colorblind-safe (avoids red-green), but not an explicitly CB-optimised palette.
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout() used, 5.5×5 figsize, top/right spines removed, scatter points rasterized — space is well used with no obvious whitespace or overlap issues.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes all visual elements with specific threshold values; gene annotations use generic indices (g0, g1…) rather than gene symbols, which a reviewer would likely flag.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is genuine vector output from Matplotlib PDF backend (161 KB, 1 page) but fonts are Type 3 with Custom encoding — text is not selectable; fonttype=42 was not set.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (161 KB), figure.png (86 KB), figure.svg (109 KB, bonus), caption.tex (583 B), gen_figure.py.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states axes, colour coding with explicit threshold numbers (log2FC > 1, -log10p > 2), describes dashed threshold lines, and notes top-5 labelling — provides a specific numerical finding.

overall_strengths: Complete file set with all required outputs plus an SVG; caption is detailed and numerically specific.
Correct scientific implementation: three-category classification, threshold lines, and top-5 annotation all match the brief.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' — Type 3 fonts in the PDF make text non-selectable and non-editable, a standard journal requirement.
Default DejaVu Sans font and no explicit colorblind-safe palette (e.g. IBM/Wong) — both are common reviewer complaints for publication-ready figures.
